summary: CR2 (Canon RAW) image orientation is incorrectly reported as SKEncodedOrigin.TopLeft instead of SKEncodedOrigin.LeftBottom by SKCodec.EncodedOrigin on Linux and macOS, causing thumbnails to be generated with wrong rotation.

bugSignals.errorMessage: codec.EncodedOrigin returns SKEncodedOrigin.TopLeft (1) instead of SKEncodedOrigin.LeftBottom (8) for a CR2 file with EXIF orientation Rotate 270 CW

stepsToReproduce: Load a CR2 file whose EXIF orientation is 'Left side, bottom (Rotate 270 CW)' using SKCodec.Create()
Observe it returns SKEncodedOrigin.TopLeft (default) instead of SKEncodedOrigin.LeftBottom

repoLinks.description: Sample CR2 file uploaded by reporter (LV1_3765.CR2.zip)
Related issue by same author: JPEG orientation wrong for some images (closed/completed)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The EncodedOrigin property directly calls sk_codec_get_origin (native). No changes to this code path in recent releases suggest the issue is still present.

analysis.summary: SKCodec.EncodedOrigin returns TopLeft (default) for a CR2 RAW image that has EXIF orientation 8 (LeftBottom / Rotate 270 CW). The C# binding correctly calls the native sk_codec_get_origin function, so the root cause is in the native Skia RAW codec's handling of EXIF orientation tags inside CR2 files. CR2 uses an EXIF-based orientation tag but Skia's dng_sdk or piex-based RAW decoder may not parse this tag correctly for Canon CR2 files, or may return the default when the orientation sub-IFD is located at a Canon-specific offset.

codeInvestigation.finding: EncodedOrigin property calls SkiaApi.sk_codec_get_origin(Handle) directly with no transformation — the C# layer is correct and transparent
sk_codec_get_origin is a direct P/Invoke to the native symbol. No caching or conversion applied. If native returns 1 (TopLeft), that is what C# sees.
SKEncodedOrigin enum: TopLeft=1 (same as Default), LeftBottom=8. Matches EXIF standard. The enum mapping is correct.

keySignals.text: ExifTool correctly shows the orientation as: Orientation = Left side, bottom (Rotate 270 CW)
keySignals.interpretation: Third-party tool correctly reads EXIF orientation tag from the CR2 file, confirming orientation data is present. The fault is in Skia's RAW codec parsing, not missing EXIF data.

keySignals.text: The image orientation is evaluated as SKEncodedOrigin.TopLeft
keySignals.interpretation: TopLeft is the SKEncodedOrigin.Default value (1). Skia's RAW codec is falling back to the default, not reading the EXIF tag.
Bug is cross-platform (Linux and macOS) suggesting it is in the native Skia codec logic, not a platform-specific interop issue.

analysis.rationale: The C# layer correctly passes through what the native sk_codec_get_origin returns. The bug is in Skia's native RAW/CR2 codec failing to read the EXIF orientation tag. The same reporter had a similar JPEG orientation issue (#1517) which was resolved. CR2 is a proprietary Canon RAW format; Skia uses piex (preview extractor) or dng_sdk, which may not handle Canon-specific EXIF IFD layouts for orientation. This is a native codec limitation, not a SkiaSharp binding bug.
workarounds: Use a dedicated EXIF library (e.g., MetadataExtractor NuGet package) to read the orientation tag from the CR2 file independently, then apply the rotation manually using SKBitmap.RotateCW() or a matrix transform.
Use ImageMagick or ExifTool to pre-rotate the image and then process with SkiaSharp.
nextQuestions: Does the issue affect all Canon CR2 files or only specific camera models/firmware?
Does the issue affect DNG files or only Canon's proprietary CR2?
Which version of Skia (milestone) is bundled with SkiaSharp 2.88.8 — does upstream Skia have a fix?

resolution.hypothesis: Skia's RAW codec (piex/dng_sdk) does not read the EXIF orientation tag from Canon CR2 files, falling back to the default TopLeft orientation. A fix would require updating the Skia RAW codec to handle the Canon EXIF orientation sub-IFD.

proposals.title: Use MetadataExtractor to read EXIF orientation
proposals.description: Read EXIF orientation using the MetadataExtractor NuGet package, then apply rotation as a workaround until the upstream Skia RAW codec is fixed.

proposals.title: Fix in upstream Skia RAW codec
proposals.description: Investigate and fix Skia's RAW codec (likely in src/codec/SkRawCodec.cpp or piex) to properly parse the EXIF orientation tag from Canon CR2 files and return the correct SkEncodedOrigin.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use MetadataExtractor to read EXIF orientation
resolution.recommendedReason: A native Skia fix requires upstream cooperation and substantial investigation of the proprietary CR2 format. The workaround unblocks the reporter immediately.

actionability.reason: Real bug with sample code and external image file. Root cause is in the native Skia RAW codec which is inaccessible without the checked-out submodule. Needs investigation against actual CR2 file to confirm behavior and trace through native codec.

actions.description: Apply bug, SkiaSharp, Linux and macOS labels
Acknowledge bug, explain root cause hypothesis, provide workaround using MetadataExtractor
Link to related JPEG orientation issue by same reporter
